scope.claim_limit: Synthetic curved-object/non-urban mechanism stress only; public curved-object or non-urban dataset validation is still required before promoting Quadric-LO to T0.

fallback_on.note: Quadric-LO: quadric-surface (implicit q=xAx+bx+c) representation with point-to-quadric Taubin-distance residuals and plane fallback enabled; constant-velocity prior, no GT seed. mean_quadric/plane_corr=1242/12 plane_fallback_ratio=0.0094

fallback_off.note: Quadric-LO: quadric-surface (implicit q=xAx+bx+c) representation with point-to-quadric Taubin-distance residuals and plane fallback disabled; constant-velocity prior, no GT seed. mean_quadric/plane_corr=1245/0 plane_fallback_ratio=0.0000

conclusion: On the synthetic orchard-like curved-object stress, Quadric-LO uses almost exclusively point-to-quadric constraints. Disabling plane fallback keeps ATE and RPE effectively unchanged, confirming that the evidence is dominated by the quadric path rather than by the point-to-plane fallback. KISS-ICP remains the sanity reference on this easy synthetic odometry geometry.
